title: Heleniastrum autumnale
intro: Heleniastrum autumnale, also known as Autumn Gentian or Yellow Gentian, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region and China. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Heleniastrum autumnale include gentiopicroside, gentiacauleine, and other iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include extracts, capsules, and teas, often used to relieve symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Heleniastrum autumnale is classified as a plant species within the family Boraginaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the borage family. The genus Heleniastrum, to which this species belongs, is a small group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. Within the Boraginaceae family, Heleniastrum autumnale is part of the tribe Boragineae. The plant's classification reflects its relationships to other flowering plants in the region, as well as its distinct characteristics. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
